_comment: Dangerous shell command patterns for dangerous-command-gate.js. Add project-specific patterns here. Each entry has: name (display label), pattern (JS regex string), severity (critical|high|medium), reason (why it is dangerous). The hook checks the Bash command against these patterns in order and stops at the first match.
_enforce_note: Set HORUS_ENFORCE=1 to activate block mode. In warn mode (default) the hook prints a warning to stderr and lets the command proceed. In block mode the hook exits with code 2 and aborts the tool call.

patterns.name: rm recursive force
patterns.reason: Recursive forced deletion is irreversible. Deleting files requires Ahmed's explicit approval per Agent Runtime Guard policy.

patterns.name: rm no-preserve-root
patterns.reason: Removes the safeguard preventing deletion of root filesystem. Never allowed.

patterns.name: git push force
patterns.reason: Force push can overwrite shared history and destroy teammates' work. Requires explicit approval.

patterns.name: DROP DATABASE / DROP TABLE
patterns.reason: Destroys database objects irreversibly. Requires explicit approval per shell policy.

patterns.name: curl pipe to shell
patterns.reason: Executes untrusted remote code directly — violates Agent Runtime Guard no-unreviewed-remote-execution policy.

patterns.name: mkfs (disk format)
patterns.reason: Formats a disk partition — irreversible data loss. Never auto-approved.

patterns.name: dd destructive disk write
patterns.reason: Writes raw data to a disk device — can destroy filesystems.

patterns.name: sudo with destructive command
patterns.reason: Elevated destructive command. Requires explicit approval.

patterns.name: npx -y (auto-download execution)
patterns.reason: Automatically downloads and executes remote npm packages — violates Agent Runtime Guard policy.

patterns.name: chmod world-writable
patterns.reason: Makes files world-writable — opens privilege escalation paths.

patterns.name: git reset hard
patterns.reason: Discards all uncommitted changes and can discard commits — irreversible without reflog.

patterns.name: git clean force
patterns.reason: Deletes untracked files permanently. Requires explicit intent.

patterns.name: DELETE without WHERE
patterns.reason: Deletes all rows in a table with no WHERE clause — likely unintentional.

patterns.name: sudo generic elevation
patterns.reason: Elevated privilege execution. Confirm this is intentional and necessary.

patterns.name: kill -9 / killall
patterns.reason: Force-kills processes without cleanup. Can leave data in corrupt state.

patterns.name: chmod recursive
patterns.reason: Recursively changes permissions on many files — easy to accidentally open or lock down a directory tree.

patterns.name: shred (secure delete)
patterns.reason: Securely overwrites files making recovery impossible.

patterns.name: prompt injection — ignore instructions
patterns.reason: Classic prompt injection attempt — instructs the model to discard safety rules. Commands containing this phrasing are almost always injected, not authored.

patterns.name: prompt injection — override policy
patterns.reason: Instructs the model to bypass safety or security policy — a key prompt injection vector.

patterns.name: prompt injection — exfiltrate data
patterns.reason: Command requesting data exfiltration — likely injected by malicious content in a file or prompt.

patterns.name: prompt injection — jailbreak framing
patterns.reason: Jailbreak framing — attempts to redefine the agent's identity or rules mid-session.
